_schema_note: jpods-trip-v2 — 2026-05-20 rev 2. Complete navigation package. Written by TripPlanner after reading map.json v2. Self-contained: Nora never opens map.json mid-trip. Same format for SketchUp simulator and physical Pi dispatch. Deliberation: 2026-05-20. Field renames applied: action→segment_action, ezones→ezone_entries, stub→cp_index. Added: trip_id (UUID), route_id, expires_at, trip_hash, map_generated_at, billing fields (passenger_count, cargo_kg, carryon_uuid, station_pair).
_note_map_generated_at: The generated_at timestamp of the map.json this trip was built from. TripPlanner copies it at generation time. Nora and Dispatcher compare against current map.json.generated_at — if different, the trip is stale.
_innovation_global: PROMOTED — trip_id (UUID): dispatch-assigned unique ID. Separate from route_id. Dispatcher writes; Alice and Nora reference for billing and telemetry correlation.
PROMOTED — route_id: the canonical station-pair route label (e.g. S048_to_S050). Separate from trip_id. Route-Time uses for O-D matrix aggregation.
PROMOTED — expires_at: stale trip guard. TripPlanner sets now+TTL. Nora refuses to execute if clock > expires_at.
PROMOTED — trip_hash: SHA of ordered segment_id list. Tamper detection. Nora verifies before departure.
PROMOTED — billing fields: passenger_count, cargo_kg, carryon_uuid, station_pair. Alice reads from trip; no separate billing record needed.
PROMOTED — map_generated_at: stale map detection. TripPlanner copies from map.json at generation time.
PROMOTED — segment_action (was 'action'): renamed to avoid collision with generic 'action' in dispatch protocol.
PROMOTED — ezone_entries (was 'ezones'): renamed to reflect per-trip nature vs. network-wide ezone registry.
PROMOTED — cp_index (was 'stub'): renamed to match noelle_features.json stub_pair numbering.
OPEN — ALLIE: multi-hop trips (more than one inter-station connection). Current hops:1 only. Route-Time O-D matrix needs multi-hop. Add hops[] array with one entry per inter-station connection?
OPEN — NOELLE: should TripPlanner validate that every segment_id exists in the current map.json before writing trip.json? Catch topology drift before Nora sees it.

OPEN — ALLIE: Route-Time O-D matrix population. TripPlanner writes one trip.json per O-D pair; Route-Time reads the set. No separate route computation needed — confirm this is the intended architecture.

trips._note_trip_id: UUID assigned by Dispatcher at dispatch time. Null in pre-computed route files. Alice and Nora use this as the billing and telemetry correlation key.
trips._note_route_id: Canonical route label: {origin}_{to}_{destination}. TripPlanner writes. Route-Time aggregates by route_id for O-D matrix. Stable across multiple dispatches of the same route.
trips._note_hops: Count of inter-station connections in this trip. hops=1 = direct. hops>1 = multi-hop (not yet implemented). Multi-hop requires a hops[] array; see _innovation_global.
trips._note_expires_at: ISO-8601 expiry. TripPlanner sets to generated_at + TTL (e.g. 4 hours for pre-computed; 30 minutes for live dispatch). Nora refuses to execute if current time > expires_at. Null = no expiry (SketchUp simulator only).
trips._note_trip_hash: SHA-256 of the concatenated ordered segment IDs (e.g. SHA('S048.gw_platform|S048.gw_platform_out|...')). Nora verifies before departure. Tamper detection. Null = not yet computed (draft).
trips._note_total_length: Sum of all segment length_mm values. TripPlanner calculates at write time. Nora uses for progress reporting (mm_traveled / total_length_mm).
trips._note_estimated_time: Nominal travel time at average speed. TripPlanner sums nominal_time_s from map.json lines. Alice uses for schedule. Dispatcher uses for headway planning.
trips._note_estimated_energy: Sum of energy_model.estimated_wh across all segments from map.json. Alice bills by energy. Solar offset credited against this.
trips._note_passenger_capacity: From station template in noelle_features.json. Alice enforces booking limit.
trips._note_passenger_count: Actual passengers on this dispatch. Dispatcher or booking API writes at dispatch time. Alice bills by this count. Null in pre-computed route files.
trips._note_cargo_kg: Actual cargo weight in kg on this dispatch. Affects energy cost calculation and fare. Dispatcher writes at dispatch time. Null = no cargo / not yet assigned.
trips._note_carryon_uuid: MyCarryOn UUID of the trip requestor. Alice links billing record to this identity. Null in pre-computed route files and internal maintenance trips.
trips._note_station_pair: Canonical fare-zone key for Alice. Format: origin_station_id:destination_station_id. Alice looks up base fare from this key. Always populated — even in pre-computed files.

segments._note: First intra-station segment. Pod departs S048 platform. All physical data embedded from map.json — Nora reads this record, navigates, never queries map.json.
segments._note_cumulative_mm: Distance from trip start to beginning of this segment. Nora reports position as cumulative_mm + mm_into_segment.
segments._note_segment_action: depart | transit | arrive | dwell. Nora uses to trigger door open/close, passenger notification, cargo lock/unlock. TripPlanner derives from role. Renamed from 'action' (deliberation 2026-05-20) to avoid collision with dispatch protocol 'action' field.
segments._note_dwell_time_s: Non-zero only for gw_platform at origin (boarding) and destination (alighting). TripPlanner sets from noelle_features.json dwell_time_s.

segments._note_endPoint: Stub pair tip — this is the CP. Connects to inter-station segment startPoint.

segments._note_grade: Stub pair lifts from station deck to guideway beam height — significant grade. Nora needs extra motor torque here.

segments._note_cp_index: cp_index is the stub_pair number (0-based) on this station. Renamed from 'stub' (deliberation 2026-05-20).

segments._note: Abbreviated — full Bezier path has ~212 segments at 2m resolution. Noelle generates all from followme.json track data.

ezone_entries._note: Ezone entries relevant to this segment, copied from map.json at plan time. Nora checks these without opening map.json. Renamed from 'ezones' (deliberation 2026-05-20) to reflect per-trip nature.
ezone_entries._note_entry_dist_mm: Distance along this segment where the ezone begins. Nora starts deceleration ramp at entry_dist_mm - approach_decel_mm.

segments._note_endPoint: Populated by Noelle from map.json. Placeholder zeros here in draft.

segments._note: Terminal segment. Pod stops here. speedMax 50 = slow crawl into platform. dwell_time_s 30 = passenger alighting + boarding window.

physical_json_check._note: Before Dispatcher assigns this trip to Nora, check physical.json for known anomalies on any segment. Noelle reads physical.json; if severity >= moderate on any segment in the trip, block the assignment and flag to operator.
